item.title: Orbital and magnetic ordering in the weak ferromagnet vanadium trifluoride ${\mathrm{VF}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Reinhard K. Kremer, Florian Kraus, Patrick Woidy, Gwilherm Nénert, Christina Drahten, and Bachir Ouladdiaf

We report on an investigation of the low-temperature structural, thermal, and magnetic properties of the binary trifluoride ${\mathrm{VF}}_{3}$ by temperature-dependent magnetization, heat capacity, electron paramagnetic resonance, and synchrotron powder and neutron powder diffraction measurements. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054442] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Reinhard K. Kremer, Florian Kraus, Patrick Woidy, Gwilherm Nénert, Christina Drahten, and Bachir Ouladdiaf

We report on an investigation of the low-temperature structural, thermal, and magnetic properties of the binary trifluoride VF3 by temperature-dependent magnetization, heat capacity, electron paramagnetic resonance, and synchrotron powder and neutron powder diffraction measurements. At room temperat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054442] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital and magnetic ordering in the weak ferromagnet vanadium trifluoride ${\mathrm{VF}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Reinhard K. Kremer, Florian Kraus, Patrick Woidy, Gwilherm Nénert, Christina Drahten, and Bachir Ouladdiaf

item.title: Pressure-induced enhancement of magnetism in ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$, a layered magnetic topological insulator, provides an ideal platform for studying the pressure-tuned interplay between magnetism and topology. Using first-principles density functional theory and Monte Carlo simulations, we examine its structura…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054443] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

Mn2Bi2Te5, a layered magnetic topological insulator, provides an ideal platform for studying the pressure-tuned interplay between magnetism and topology. Using first-principles density functional theory and Monte Carlo simulations, we examine its structural, magnetic, and electronic evolution under …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054443] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-induced enhancement of magnetism in ${\mathrm{Mn}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Xiaoteng Huang, Wenfeng Wu, Xianlong Wang, Yonggang Li, Liangjian Zou, and Zhi Zeng

item.title: Higher-order topological superconductivity in type-II time-reversal symmetric Weyl semimetals with a hybrid pairing
item.description:
Author(s): Junkang Huang, Z. D. Wang, and Tao Zhou

We employed the self-consistent method on a two-orbital type-II time-reversal-symmetric Weyl semimetal, revealing a hybrid pairing of singlet $s$ wave and triplet $p$ wave. We present a detailed analysis of the normal-state electronic structure and the self-consistent results. Our findings indicate …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054523] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junkang Huang, Z. D. Wang, and Tao Zhou

We employed the self-consistent method on a two-orbital type-II time-reversal-symmetric Weyl semimetal, revealing a hybrid pairing of singlet s wave and triplet p wave. We present a detailed analysis of the normal-state electronic structure and the self-consistent results. Our findings indicate that…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054523] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Higher-order topological superconductivity in type-II time-reversal symmetric Weyl semimetals with a hybrid pairing
item.dc:creator: Junkang Huang, Z. D. Wang, and Tao Zhou

item.title: Unveiling the superconducting scenario in multiphase superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ based on space-group symmetry analysis and DFT calculations
item.description:
Author(s): V. G. Yarzhemsky, E. A. Teplyakov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov

Despite of the low transition temperature, the recently identified superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ has garnered significant interest due to its unique symmetry and magnetic characteristics, particularly the existence of two superconducting phases under a magnetic field, one of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054524] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. G. Yarzhemsky, E. A. Teplyakov, S. V. Eremeev, and E. V. Chulkov

Despite of the low transition temperature, the recently identified superconductor CeRh2As2 has garnered significant interest due to its unique symmetry and magnetic characteristics, particularly the existence of two superconducting phases under a magnetic field, one of which exceeds the Pauli-Clogst…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054524] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Unveiling the superconducting scenario in multiphase superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ based on space-group symmetry analysis and DFT calculations

item.title: High bosonic Bott index and transport of multiband topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Kai-Tao Huang and X. S. Wang

Magnons are bosonic quasiparticles in magnetically ordered systems. Bosonic Bott index has been affirmed as a real-space topological invariant for a two-band ferromagnetic model. In this work, we theoretically investigate the topology and transport of magnons in a multiband bosonic kagome ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064439] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai-Tao Huang and X. S. Wang

Magnons are bosonic quasiparticles in magnetically ordered systems. Bosonic Bott index has been affirmed as a real-space topological invariant for a two-band ferromagnetic model. In this work, we theoretically investigate the topology and transport of magnons in a multiband bosonic kagome ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064439] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: High bosonic Bott index and transport of multiband topological magnons

item.title: Multilayer stacking ferroelectricity in two-dimensional materials with Bravais lattice symmetry: Theory and applications
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaqi Xin, Yaguang Guo, and Qian Wang

The sliding of van der Waals (vdW) bilayers can induce electric polarization in certain nonpolar two-dimensional (2D) materials. However, this mechanism does not apply to 2D materials with Bravais lattice symmetry (e.g., graphene), where the atomic structure and lattice symmetry have the same point …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075310] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaqi Xin, Yaguang Guo, and Qian Wang

The sliding of van der Waals (vdW) bilayers can induce electric polarization in certain nonpolar two-dimensional (2D) materials. However, this mechanism does not apply to 2D materials with Bravais lattice symmetry (e.g., graphene), where the atomic structure and lattice symmetry have the same point …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075310] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Multilayer stacking ferroelectricity in two-dimensional materials with Bravais lattice symmetry: Theory and applications
item.dc:creator: Jiaqi Xin, Yaguang Guo, and Qian Wang

item.title: Effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism in the annealed kagome magnet FeGe
item.description:
Author(s): Ao Zhang, Run Yang, Yiyuan Zhu, Zhixiang Shi, Xueliang Wu, Aifeng Wang, Bingke Ji, Yaomin Dai, and Jianzhou Zhao

In this work, combined with the optical spectroscopy and the first-principles calculations, we investigated the effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism of FeGe annealed at different temperatures. Our observations reveal that the sample annealed at $320{\phantom{\rule{0.16em}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085146] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ao Zhang, Run Yang, Yiyuan Zhu, Zhixiang Shi, Xueliang Wu, Aifeng Wang, Bingke Ji, Yaomin Dai, and Jianzhou Zhao

In this work, combined with the optical spectroscopy and the first-principles calculations, we investigated the effect of lattice distortion on the band structure and magnetism of FeGe annealed at different temperatures. Our observations reveal that the sample annealed at 320∘C experienced dramatic …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085146] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Interplay of charge carrier and antiferromagnetic order in metallic triangular lattice ${\mathrm{GdZn}}_{3}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jiesen Guo, Fan Yang, Yuzhou He, Xinyang Liu, Zheng Deng, Qinghua Zhang, Xiancheng Wang, Xianlei Sheng, Wei Li, Changqing Jin, and Kan Zhao

We investigate a hexagonal ${\mathrm{ScAl}}_{3}{\mathrm{C}}_{3}$-type antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{GdZn}}_{3}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$ single crystal. Compared with antiferromagnetic topological material ${\mathrm{Eu}\mathit{M}}_{2}{X}_{2}$ $(M=\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Cd}; X=\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{As})$, the ${\math…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085147] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiesen Guo, Fan Yang, Yuzhou He, Xinyang Liu, Zheng Deng, Qinghua Zhang, Xiancheng Wang, Xianlei Sheng, Wei Li, Changqing Jin, and Kan Zhao

We investigate a hexagonal ScAl3C3-type antiferromagnet GdZn3P3 single crystal. Compared with antiferromagnetic topological material EuM2X2 (M=Zn, Cd; X=P, As), the GdZn3P3 features an additional ZnP3 trigonal planar units. Notably, single-crystal x-ray diffraction analysis reveals that Zn and P ato…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085147] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of charge carrier and antiferromagnetic order in metallic triangular lattice ${\mathrm{GdZn}}_{3}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Jiesen Guo, Fan Yang, Yuzhou He, Xinyang Liu, Zheng Deng, Qinghua Zhang, Xiancheng Wang, Xianlei Sheng, Wei Li, Changqing Jin, and Kan Zhao

item.title: Fragility of antichiral edge states in a modified Haldane model
item.description:
Author(s): Shuguang Cheng

Antichiral edge states, proposed as protected copropagating modes in the modified Haldane model, have since been studied in diverse systems. Through numerical calculation, we reveal that such antichiral edge states are unstable to disorder: they exhibit a shorter localization length than the bulk st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085303] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuguang Cheng

Antichiral edge states, proposed as protected copropagating modes in the modified Haldane model, have since been studied in diverse systems. Through numerical calculation, we reveal that such antichiral edge states are unstable to disorder: they exhibit a shorter localization length than the bulk st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085303] Published Wed Feb 25, 2026

item.title: Tunable interplay of strain and magnetism in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

We report our direct investigation on a built-in strain and its effect on magnetic properties in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructure. With variation of Co thickness, tensile strain is built in monolayer ${\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ ranging from 0.7% to 1.9%. Magneto-optical Kerr microscopy reve…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054440] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

We report our direct investigation on a built-in strain and its effect on magnetic properties in Co/MoS2 heterostructure. With variation of Co thickness, tensile strain is built in monolayer MoS2 ranging from 0.7% to 1.9%. Magneto-optical Kerr microscopy reveals distinct changes in microscopic magne…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054440] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable interplay of strain and magnetism in $\mathrm{Co}/{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Qoimatul Mustaghfiroh, Su Jin Kim, Ikhwan Nur Rahman, Anabil Gayen, Fathiya Rahmani, Min Choi, Yeon Suk Choi, Seung-Young Park, Chuyang Liu, Yujing Zhang, Hyun Seok Lee, and Dong-Hyun Kim

item.title: Sliding engineering spin-valley-layer coupling and altermagnetism in bilayer antiferromagnetic honeycomb lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Wen-Xin Jiang, Zhen-Hao Gong, Yuantao Chen, Zhigang Gui, and Li Huang

Valley polarization and altermagnetism are two emerging fundamental phenomena in condensed matter physics, offering unique opportunities for information encoding in novel energy-efficient devices. However, achieving electrical control over these properties in a single material remains a significant …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054441] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026

Valley polarization and altermagnetism are two emerging fundamental phenomena in condensed matter physics, offering unique opportunities for information encoding in novel energy-efficient devices. However, achieving electrical control over these properties in a single material remains a significant …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054441] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026

item.title: Spin-polarized Dirac points and near-flat bands in ${\mathrm{FePd}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}$ with a one-dimensional ferromagnetic zigzag chain
item.description:
Author(s): Pengfei Ding, Huihui He, Bingxian Shi, Zhixiao He, Xiaoxu Gao, Peng Cheng, Kai Liu, and Shancai Wang

One-dimensional zigzag chains offer a striking platform for hosting Dirac fermions protected by glide reflection symmetry, along with nearly flat electronic bands. Though rich in theoretical promise, these lattices are mostly found as structural fragments rather than in fully formed bulk crystals. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075151] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pengfei Ding, Huihui He, Bingxian Shi, Zhixiao He, Xiaoxu Gao, Peng Cheng, Kai Liu, and Shancai Wang

One-dimensional zigzag chains offer a striking platform for hosting Dirac fermions protected by glide reflection symmetry, along with nearly flat electronic bands. Though rich in theoretical promise, these lattices are mostly found as structural fragments rather than in fully formed bulk crystals. I…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075151] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026

item.title: Magnetotransport across Weyl semimetal grain boundaries
item.description:
Author(s): Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz

A clean interface between two Weyl semimetals features a universal, field-linear tunnel magnetoconductance of $({e}^{2}/h){N}_{\mathrm{ho}}$ per magnetic flux quantum, where ${N}_{\mathrm{ho}}$ is the number of chirality-preserving topological interface Fermi arcs. In this work we show that the line…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075152] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz

A clean interface between two Weyl semimetals features a universal, field-linear tunnel magnetoconductance of (e2/h)Nho per magnetic flux quantum, where Nho is the number of chirality-preserving topological interface Fermi arcs. In this work we show that the linearity of the magnetoconductance is ro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075152] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetotransport across Weyl semimetal grain boundaries
item.dc:creator: Haoyang Tian, Vatsal Dwivedi, Adam Yanis Chaou, and Maxim Breitkreiz

item.title: Quantized nonlinear transport and its breakdown in Fermi gases with Berry curvature
item.description:
Author(s): Fan Yang and Xingyu Li

Quantized transport not only exists in gapped topological states but also in metallic states. Recently, Kane proposed a quantized nonlinear conductance in ballistic metals whose value is determined by the Euler characteristic of the Fermi sea [Kane et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 076801 (2022)]. In th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075431] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fan Yang and Xingyu Li

Quantized transport not only exists in gapped topological states but also in metallic states. Recently, Kane proposed a quantized nonlinear conductance in ballistic metals whose value is determined by the Euler characteristic of the Fermi sea [Kane et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 076801 (2022)]. In th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075431] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026

item.title: Low spin-flip probability of Kondo scattering in Cu channels with dilute Fe impurities
item.description:
Author(s): Xingyu Shen and Yi Ji

It is commonly accepted that the Kondo effect is associated with a substantial probability of spin flip owing to the antiferromagnetic exchange interaction. An accurate experimental determination of the Kondo spin flip probability is of fundamental interest. In this work, we determine this quantity …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085141] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xingyu Shen and Yi Ji

It is commonly accepted that the Kondo effect is associated with a substantial probability of spin flip owing to the antiferromagnetic exchange interaction. An accurate experimental determination of the Kondo spin flip probability is of fundamental interest. In this work, we determine this quantity …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085141] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026

item.title: Electrically tunable RKKY interaction in Dirac semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Han Li, Zhuo-Hua Chen, Wei Luo, Hou-Jian Duan, Rong Ma, Rui-Qiang Wang, and Ming-Xun Deng

We develop a theory based on nonequilibrium Green's functions to describe indirect magnetic interactions in systems subjected to external fields. Using this framework, we investigate the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction in Dirac semimetals under the influence of both electric and mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085145] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Han Li, Zhuo-Hua Chen, Wei Luo, Hou-Jian Duan, Rong Ma, Rui-Qiang Wang, and Ming-Xun Deng

We develop a theory based on nonequilibrium Green's functions to describe indirect magnetic interactions in systems subjected to external fields. Using this framework, we investigate the Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) interaction in Dirac semimetals under the influence of both electric and mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085145] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrically tunable RKKY interaction in Dirac semimetals

item.title: Persistent charge and spin currents in a ferromagnetic Hatano-Nelson ring
item.description:
Author(s): Sourav Karmakar, Sudin Ganguly, and Santanu K. Maiti

We investigate persistent charge and spin currents in a ferromagnetic Hatano-Nelson ring with anti-Hermitian intradimer hopping, where nonreciprocal hopping generates a synthetic magnetic flux and drives a non-Hermitian Aharonov-Bohm effect. The system supports both real and imaginary persistent cur…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085433] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sourav Karmakar, Sudin Ganguly, and Santanu K. Maiti

We investigate persistent charge and spin currents in a ferromagnetic Hatano-Nelson ring with anti-Hermitian intradimer hopping, where nonreciprocal hopping generates a synthetic magnetic flux and drives a non-Hermitian Aharonov-Bohm effect. The system supports both real and imaginary persistent cur…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085433] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Persistent charge and spin currents in a ferromagnetic Hatano-Nelson ring
item.dc:creator: Sourav Karmakar, Sudin Ganguly, and Santanu K. Maiti

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Tunnel-like transport and extremely large magnetoresistance in an all-metal junction incorporating the altermagnet ${\mathrm{KV}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jing-Jing He, Ling-Xiao Liu, Yan-Dong Guo, Jia-Ren Yuan, Xiao-Hong Yan, and Stefano Sanvito

Here, the authors design an all-metal altermagnetic junction based on KV2Se2O that exhibits tunnel-like transport, enabled by symmetry-driven spin selectivity. Suppressed electronic coupling between electrodes and spacer allows spin-neutral currents to generate exceptionally large tunneling magnetoresistance. This behavior, fundamentally distinct from that in conventional ferromagnetic tunnel junctions, suggests a new avenue for high-performance antiferromagnetic devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060410] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing-Jing He, Ling-Xiao Liu, Yan-Dong Guo, Jia-Ren Yuan, Xiao-Hong Yan, and Stefano Sanvito

Here, the authors design an all-metal altermagnetic junction based on KV2Se2O that exhibits tunnel-like transport, enabled by symmetry-driven spin selectivity. Suppressed electronic coupling between electrodes and spacer allows spin-neutral currents to generate exceptionally large tunneling magnetoresistance. This behavior, fundamentally distinct from that in conventional ferromagnetic tunnel junctions, suggests a new avenue for high-performance antiferromagnetic devices.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L060410] Published Tue Feb 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunnel-like transport and extremely large magnetoresistance in an all-metal junction incorporating the altermagnet ${\mathrm{KV}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$
item.dc:creator: Jing-Jing He, Ling-Xiao Liu, Yan-Dong Guo, Jia-Ren Yuan, Xiao-Hong Yan, and Stefano Sanvito

item.title: Two-dimensional quasilayered hexagonal ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{N}}_{2}$ manipulated by temperature, strain, electric field, and magnetic field: Antiferromagnetic higher-order topological Mott state and in-plane valley polarization effect
item.description:
Author(s): Zixuan Zhang, Xibin Liu, Lixiu Guan, Xiaobiao Liu, Xiangru Kong, and Linyang Li

Exploring the coexistence of multiple quantum states in two-dimensional materials is a major goal in condensed matter physics. In this study, we theoretically predicted that the quasilayered hexagonal ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{N}}_{2}$ (h-${\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{N}}_{2}$) monolayer simultaneously …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054436] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zixuan Zhang, Xibin Liu, Lixiu Guan, Xiaobiao Liu, Xiangru Kong, and Linyang Li

Exploring the coexistence of multiple quantum states in two-dimensional materials is a major goal in condensed matter physics. In this study, we theoretically predicted that the quasilayered hexagonal V2N2 (h-V2N2) monolayer simultaneously hosts metal-insulator antiferromagnetic (AFM) Mott phase tra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054436] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-dimensional quasilayered hexagonal ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{N}}_{2}$ manipulated by temperature, strain, electric field, and magnetic field: Antiferromagnetic higher-order topological Mott state and in-plane valley polarization effect
item.dc:creator: Zixuan Zhang, Xibin Liu, Lixiu Guan, Xiaobiao Liu, Xiangru Kong, and Linyang Li
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Platform for zero-field isolated skyrmions: $4d/\mathrm{Co}$ atomic bilayers on Re(0001)
item.description:
Author(s): Moinak Ghosh, Stefan Heinze, and Souvik Paul

Here, the authors identify atomic bilayers of Rh/Co and Pd/Co on the Re(0001) surface as a promising platform to realize isolated skyrmions in the absence of an external magnetic field. Using an atomistic spin model, including higher-order multi-spin exchange interactions and completely parameterized from density functional theory, they show that zero-field isolated skyrmions emerge with diameters on the order of 10–20 nm with substantial energy barriers, indicating the possibility of experimental verification at low temperatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054437] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Moinak Ghosh, Stefan Heinze, and Souvik Paul

Here, the authors identify atomic bilayers of Rh/Co and Pd/Co on the Re(0001) surface as a promising platform to realize isolated skyrmions in the absence of an external magnetic field. Using an atomistic spin model, including higher-order multi-spin exchange interactions and completely parameterized from density functional theory, they show that zero-field isolated skyrmions emerge with diameters on the order of 10–20 nm with substantial energy barriers, indicating the possibility of experimental verification at low temperatures.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054437] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Platform for zero-field isolated skyrmions: $4d/\mathrm{Co}$ atomic bilayers on Re(0001)
item.dc:creator: Moinak Ghosh, Stefan Heinze, and Souvik Paul
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Exceptionally large winding number of a finite-size topological superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Satoshi Ikegaya, Shingo Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Asano

We study finite-size-induced topological phenomena in unconventional superconductors. Specifically, we focus on a thin film with a persistent spin texture, fabricated on a high-${T}_{\text{c}}$ cuprate ${d}_{xy}$-wave superconductors. In two-dimensional ${d}_{xy}$-wave superconductors, flat-band And…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054519] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Satoshi Ikegaya, Shingo Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Asano

We study finite-size-induced topological phenomena in unconventional superconductors. Specifically, we focus on a thin film with a persistent spin texture, fabricated on a high-Tc cuprate dxy-wave superconductors. In two-dimensional dxy-wave superconductors, flat-band Andreev bound states appear at …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054519] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Exceptionally large winding number of a finite-size topological superconductor
item.dc:creator: Satoshi Ikegaya, Shingo Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Asano

Author(s): Yui Ishii, Arisa Yamamoto, Alfred Q. R. Baron, Hiroshi Uchiyama, and Naoki Sato

${\mathrm{BaAl}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$ is a ferroelectric material that exhibits structural quantum criticality through chemical composition tuning. Although theoretical calculations and several diffraction experiments have suggested the involvement of a soft mode in its ferroelectric structural phas…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064112] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

Author(s): Yui Ishii, Arisa Yamamoto, Alfred Q. R. Baron, Hiroshi Uchiyama, and Naoki Sato

BaAl2O4 is a ferroelectric material that exhibits structural quantum criticality through chemical composition tuning. Although theoretical calculations and several diffraction experiments have suggested the involvement of a soft mode in its ferroelectric structural phase transition, direct experimen…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064112] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

item.title: Phase transitions of $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A$ = K, Rb, Cs) kagome compounds studied by density functional calculations
item.description:
Author(s): Hongwei Du, Zhenyi Jiang, Xiaodong Zhang, and Jiming Zheng

The precursor phenomenon of phase transitions is widely present in bulk materials, e.g., shape memory alloys, while it rarely observed in two-dimensional materials, especially in kagome lattice compounds $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A$ = K, Rb, Cs). Our calculations with density-functional…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064114] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hongwei Du, Zhenyi Jiang, Xiaodong Zhang, and Jiming Zheng

The precursor phenomenon of phase transitions is widely present in bulk materials, e.g., shape memory alloys, while it rarely observed in two-dimensional materials, especially in kagome lattice compounds AV3Sb5 (A = K, Rb, Cs). Our calculations with density-functional theory (DFT) indicated that the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064114] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Phase transitions of $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A$ = K, Rb, Cs) kagome compounds studied by density functional calculations

item.title: Ultrafast all-optical control of magnetization in the $g$-wave altermagnet CrSb
item.description:
Author(s): Zhaobo Zhou, Sangeeta Sharma, and Junjie He

The diverse nodal spin structures in $d/g/i$-wave altermagnets (AMs) may cause distinct light-induced spin responses yet remain poorly understood. Using real-time time-dependent density functional theory, we reveal that laser-induced ultrafast demagnetization dynamics in the $g$-wave AM CrSb are str…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064434] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhaobo Zhou, Sangeeta Sharma, and Junjie He

The diverse nodal spin structures in d/g/i-wave altermagnets (AMs) may cause distinct light-induced spin responses yet remain poorly understood. Using real-time time-dependent density functional theory, we reveal that laser-induced ultrafast demagnetization dynamics in the g-wave AM CrSb are strongl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064434] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

item.title: Interplay of Zeeman field, Rashba spin-orbit interaction, and superconductivity: Spin susceptibility
item.description:
Author(s): Chen Pang and Yi Zhou

We present a self-consistent theory to calculate the static and uniform spin susceptibility in superconductors under simultaneous Zeeman magnetic fields and Rashba-type spin-orbit coupling (SOC). Employing a single-band Bogoliubov-de Gennes Hamiltonian, we solve the gap equation for both conventiona…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064511] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chen Pang and Yi Zhou

We present a self-consistent theory to calculate the static and uniform spin susceptibility in superconductors under simultaneous Zeeman magnetic fields and Rashba-type spin-orbit coupling (SOC). Employing a single-band Bogoliubov-de Gennes Hamiltonian, we solve the gap equation for both conventiona…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064511] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of Zeeman field, Rashba spin-orbit interaction, and superconductivity: Spin susceptibility

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Excitation and tunneling spectra of a fractional quantum Hall system in the thin-cylinder limit
item.description:
Author(s): Jyesta M. Adhidewata and Joel E. Moore

Recent advances in scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) have allowed the experimental observation of fractional quantum Hall effect (FQHE) excited states in 2D materials. Here, the authors expand the thin torus limit using perturbation theory to include two-electron dipole-conserving hopping terms that appear as the torus increases in size. This framework allows analytical calculation of FQHE excited states, which shows good agreement with previous results while also elucidating the importance of dipole conservation in creating the observed sharp STM conductance peaks.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075143] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jyesta M. Adhidewata and Joel E. Moore

Recent advances in scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) have allowed the experimental observation of fractional quantum Hall effect (FQHE) excited states in 2D materials. Here, the authors expand the thin torus limit using perturbation theory to include two-electron dipole-conserving hopping terms that appear as the torus increases in size. This framework allows analytical calculation of FQHE excited states, which shows good agreement with previous results while also elucidating the importance of dipole conservation in creating the observed sharp STM conductance peaks.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075143] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Excitation and tunneling spectra of a fractional quantum Hall system in the thin-cylinder limit
item.dc:creator: Jyesta M. Adhidewata and Joel E. Moore
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Chirality induced spin and orbital Edelstein response in nonmagnetic B20 bulk crystals: The role of geometry, spin-orbit coupling, and finite temperature
item.description:
Author(s): Alberto Marmodoro, Ondřej Šipr, Ilja Turek, Annika Johansson, Sergiy Mankovsky, and Hubert Ebert

Chiral crystals have a noncentrosymmetric unit cell and can manifest spin-orbit coupling (SOC)-induced band splitting, even in the absence of an atomic magnetic moment. We examine the particular case of Strukturbericht B20 compounds, with AlPt as representative material, by means of first-principles…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075148] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

We examine the particular case of Strukturbericht B20 compounds, with AlPt as representative material, by means of first-principles…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075148] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

item.title: Noises in a two-channel charge Kondo model
item.description:
Author(s): T. K. T. Nguyen, J. Rech, T. Martin, and M. N. Kiselev

We investigate fluctuations of electric and heat currents, along with their cross-correlations, in a two-channel charge Kondo circuit driven by either a voltage bias or a temperature gradient applied across the weak link. The ratios of voltage-driven electric/heat noise to the applied voltage $V$ ex…

The ratios of voltage-driven electric/heat noise to the applied voltage V exhi…

item.title: Global anomalies of Green's function zeros
item.description:
Author(s): Lei Su and Ivar Martin

We study global anomalies of nonlocal effective theories proposed to describe symmetry-preserving Luttinger surfaces, i.e., the momentum-space manifolds of Green's function zeros (GFZs) at zero energy, in strongly interacting fermionic systems. In particular, we focus on the simplest possible cases …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085138] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

We study global anomalies of nonlocal effective theories proposed to describe symmetry-preserving Luttinger surfaces, i.e., the momentum-space manifolds of Green's function zeros (GFZs) at zero energy, in strongly interacting fermionic systems. In particular, we focus on the simplest possible cases …

item.title: Weyl nodes in ${\mathrm{CeRu}}_{4}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$ studied by dynamical mean field theory
item.description:
Author(s): Jorūnas Dobilas, Martin Braß, Frank T. Ebel, Silke Paschen, and Karsten Held

The heavy fermion compound ${\mathrm{CeRu}}_{4}{\mathrm{Sn}}_{6}$ was recently shown to exhibit a spontaneous nonlinear Hall effect, indicating its topological nature. This is consistent with the lack of inversion symmetry that allows for the existence of Weyl nodes. Here, we employ density function…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085140] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jorūnas Dobilas, Martin Braß, Frank T. Ebel, Silke Paschen, and Karsten Held

The heavy fermion compound CeRu4Sn6 was recently shown to exhibit a spontaneous nonlinear Hall effect, indicating its topological nature. This is consistent with the lack of inversion symmetry that allows for the existence of Weyl nodes. Here, we employ density functional theory combined with dynami…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085140] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

Recently, two unusual features were theoretically predicted for the Raman response of out-of-plane phonons in magnetic two-dimensional materials hosting massive Dirac fermions. First, the phase difference between certain Raman tensor elements was found to be quantized to $±π/2$, sensitive only to th…

First, the phase difference between certain Raman tensor elements was found to be quantized to ±π/2, sensitive only to the …

item.title: Topological phase sensitive to the boundary conditions in a purely Hermitian multileg ladder with chiral symmetry
item.description:
Author(s): Chunhui Zhang and Li Sheng

Recently, the non-Hermitian skin effect has attracted significant attention. In systems exhibiting this effect, the spectrum under the periodic boundary conditions and open boundary conditions are different, i.e., the spectrum is highly sensitive to the boundary conditions. However, most of present …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085432] Published Mon Feb 23, 2026

In systems exhibiting this effect, the spectrum under the periodic boundary conditions and open boundary conditions are different, i.e., the spectrum is highly sensitive to the boundary conditions. However, most of present …

item.title: Magnetic field tuned magnetic order and metamagnetic criticality in nonstoichiometric ${\mathrm{CeAuBi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Halyna Hodovanets, Hyunsoo Kim, Tristin Metz, Yasuyuki Nakajima, Christopher J. Eckberg, Kefeng Wang, Jie Yong, Shanta R. Saha, David Graf, Nicholas P. Butch, Thomas Vojta, and Johnpierre Paglione

We present a detailed study of magnetization, resistivity, heat capacity, and x-ray and neutron powder diffraction measurements performed on single crystals of nonstoichiometric ${\mathrm{CeAuBi}}_{2}$, Au deficiency $18%$, a strongly correlated antiferromagnet with Néel temperature ${T}_{N}=13.2$ K…

item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Halyna Hodovanets, Hyunsoo Kim, Tristin Metz, Yasuyuki Nakajima, Christopher J. Eckberg, Kefeng Wang, Jie Yong, Shanta R. Saha, David Graf, Nicholas P. Butch, Thomas Vojta, and Johnpierre Paglione

We present a detailed study of magnetization, resistivity, heat capacity, and x-ray and neutron powder diffraction measurements performed on single crystals of nonstoichiometric CeAuBi2, Au deficiency 18%, a strongly correlated antiferromagnet with Néel temperature TN=13.2 K. Field-dependent magneti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054432] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026

item.title: Attaining the ground state of kagome artificial spin ice via ultrafast site-specific laser annealing
item.description:
Author(s): D. Pecchio, S. Sahoo, V. Scagnoli, and L. J. Heyderman

Artificial spin ices (ASIs) provide a versatile platform to explore magnetic frustration and emergent phenomena. However, in kagome ASI, experimental access to the ground state remains elusive due to dynamical freezing. Here we demonstrate a deterministic and rewritable approach to attain the ground…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054434] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): D. Pecchio, S. Sahoo, V. Scagnoli, and L. J. Heyderman

Artificial spin ices (ASIs) provide a versatile platform to explore magnetic frustration and emergent phenomena. However, in kagome ASI, experimental access to the ground state remains elusive due to dynamical freezing. Here we demonstrate a deterministic and rewritable approach to attain the ground…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054434] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026

item.title: Spin liquid landscapes in the kagome lattice: A variational Monte Carlo study of the chiral Heisenberg model and experimental signatures
item.description:
Author(s): Hee Seung Kim, Hyeok-Jun Yang, Karlo Penc, and SungBin Lee

Chiral spin liquids, which break time-reversal symmetry, are of great interest due to their topological properties and fractionalized excitations (anyons). In this work, we investigate chiral spin liquids (CSL) on the kagome lattice arising from the competition between the third-nearest-neighbor Hei…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064432] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hee Seung Kim, Hyeok-Jun Yang, Karlo Penc, and SungBin Lee

Chiral spin liquids, which break time-reversal symmetry, are of great interest due to their topological properties and fractionalized excitations (anyons). In this work, we investigate chiral spin liquids (CSL) on the kagome lattice arising from the competition between the third-nearest-neighbor Hei…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064432] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026

item.description:
Author(s): Fangli Li, Pengfei Liu, Ying Fu, Lei Xu, Han Ge, Jiong Yang, Huanpeng Bu, Biaoyan Hu, Nan Zhao, Tiantian Li, Jiayue Yuan, Chen Zhang, Junzhe Liu, Zhongwen Ouyang, Ping Miao, Jieming Sheng, Andrey A. Podlesnyak, and Liusuo Wu

CePtPb hosts a distorted kagome lattice of ${\mathrm{Ce}}^{3+}$ ions, providing a clean platform to investigate how reduced local symmetry and strong spin-orbit coupling reshape frustrated magnetism. Magnetization, specific heat, and magnetocaloric effect measurements, combined with a symmetry analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064433] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fangli Li, Pengfei Liu, Ying Fu, Lei Xu, Han Ge, Jiong Yang, Huanpeng Bu, Biaoyan Hu, Nan Zhao, Tiantian Li, Jiayue Yuan, Chen Zhang, Junzhe Liu, Zhongwen Ouyang, Ping Miao, Jieming Sheng, Andrey A. Podlesnyak, and Liusuo Wu

CePtPb hosts a distorted kagome lattice of Ce3+ ions, providing a clean platform to investigate how reduced local symmetry and strong spin-orbit coupling reshape frustrated magnetism. Magnetization, specific heat, and magnetocaloric effect measurements, combined with a symmetry analysis of the singl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064433] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent quasi-one-dimensional antiferromagnetism in the distorted kagome magnet CePtPb

item.title: Entanglement signatures of gapless topological phases in a $p$-wave superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): S. Srinidhi, Shashi C. L. Srivastava, and Jayendra N. Bandyopadhyay

We explore the gapless topological phases of a $p$-wave superconductor, probing its rich topology and underlying quantum phenomena. The topology of the system is characterized by studying its entanglement properties. This study confirms the bulk-boundary correspondence in the entanglement spectrum, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064509] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Srinidhi, Shashi C. L. Srivastava, and Jayendra N. Bandyopadhyay

We explore the gapless topological phases of a p-wave superconductor, probing its rich topology and underlying quantum phenomena. The topology of the system is characterized by studying its entanglement properties. This study confirms the bulk-boundary correspondence in the entanglement spectrum, ev…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064509] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Entanglement signatures of gapless topological phases in a $p$-wave superconductor

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Optimizing superlattice bilayer graphene for a fractional Chern insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Dathan Ault-McCoy, M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Aaron Dunbrack, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, and Jennifer Cano

Bernal-stacked bilayer graphene modulated by a spatially varying superlattice potential is a highly tunable system predicted to realize isolated topological flat bands. Here, the authors identify the ideal parameter regime to host a fractional Chern insulator in this platform by analyzing the quantum geometry of the flat bands as a function of the superlattice strength, superlattice periodicity, and displacement field. The results provide an experimental roadmap for achieving fractionalization in Bernal-stacked bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075140] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dathan Ault-McCoy, M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Aaron Dunbrack, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, and Jennifer Cano

Bernal-stacked bilayer graphene modulated by a spatially varying superlattice potential is a highly tunable system predicted to realize isolated topological flat bands. Here, the authors identify the ideal parameter regime to host a fractional Chern insulator in this platform by analyzing the quantum geometry of the flat bands as a function of the superlattice strength, superlattice periodicity, and displacement field. The results provide an experimental roadmap for achieving fractionalization in Bernal-stacked bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075140] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Optimizing superlattice bilayer graphene for a fractional Chern insulator
item.dc:creator: Dathan Ault-McCoy, M. Nabil Y. Lhachemi, Aaron Dunbrack, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, and Jennifer Cano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Interaction robustness of the chiral anomaly in Weyl semimetals and Luttinger liquids from a mixed anomaly approach
item.description:
Author(s): Shuyang Wang and Jay D. Sau

The chiral anomaly in Weyl materials is one of the fascinating predictions from quantum field theory, where an electromagnetic field can lead to a topologically robust charge transfer between disconnected Fermi surfaces of the material. Here, the authors apply the recent advances in the theory of chiral anomalies in terms of emanant symmetries to demonstrate the interaction robustness of the anomaly in Weyl materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075141] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuyang Wang and Jay D. Sau

The chiral anomaly in Weyl materials is one of the fascinating predictions from quantum field theory, where an electromagnetic field can lead to a topologically robust charge transfer between disconnected Fermi surfaces of the material. Here, the authors apply the recent advances in the theory of chiral anomalies in terms of emanant symmetries to demonstrate the interaction robustness of the anomaly in Weyl materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075141] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Interaction robustness of the chiral anomaly in Weyl semimetals and Luttinger liquids from a mixed anomaly approach
item.dc:creator: Shuyang Wang and Jay D. Sau
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Phases and phase transitions of an $S=\frac{3}{2}$ chain on metallic and semimetallic surfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Bimla Danu and Fakher F. Assaad

Motivated by recent scanning tunneling microscopy experiments on chains of Co adatoms on Cu surfaces, we investigate the physics of a spin-3/2 Heisenberg chain with single-ion anisotropy $(D)$ on metallic and semimetallic surfaces. In the strong Kondo coupling $({J}_{k})$ limit, a perturbative analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075144] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bimla Danu and Fakher F. Assaad

Motivated by recent scanning tunneling microscopy experiments on chains of Co adatoms on Cu surfaces, we investigate the physics of a spin-3/2 Heisenberg chain with single-ion anisotropy (D) on metallic and semimetallic surfaces. In the strong Kondo coupling (Jk) limit, a perturbative analysis maps …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075144] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Phases and phase transitions of an $S=\frac{3}{2}$ chain on metallic and semimetallic surfaces
item.dc:creator: Bimla Danu and Fakher F. Assaad
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Half-metallic multifunctionality induced by electron doping in twisted bilayer perovskite with polar vortex patterns
item.description:
Author(s): Jiafei Pang, Tao Qian, Peng Han, Xumin Chen, and Tao Xu

Doping charges into noncoplanar spiral textures provide opportunities for the development of multifunctional memory devices. Here, we demonstrate that two-unit-cell-thick twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{SrTiO}}_{3}$ undergoes ferroelectric-to-magnetic ferroelectric and semiconducting-to-half-metallic tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075146] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiafei Pang, Tao Qian, Peng Han, Xumin Chen, and Tao Xu

Doping charges into noncoplanar spiral textures provide opportunities for the development of multifunctional memory devices. Here, we demonstrate that two-unit-cell-thick twisted bilayer SrTiO3 undergoes ferroelectric-to-magnetic ferroelectric and semiconducting-to-half-metallic transitions, thereby…

item.title: Tuning the optoelectronic properties of graphene quantum dots by BN-ring doping: A density functional theory study
item.description:
Author(s): Samayita Das and Alok Shukla

Graphene monolayer is a material with zero band gap, because of which its applications in optoelectronics are limited. The question arises, can we modify the optoelectronic properties of graphene by doping it with other atoms? Synthesis of 2D monolayer of graphene doped with hetero-atoms such as bor…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 075426] Published Fri Feb 20, 2026

Author(s): Samayita Das and Alok Shukla

Graphene monolayer is a material with zero band gap, because of which its applications in optoelectronics are limited. The question arises, can we modify the optoelectronic properties of graphene by doping it with other atoms? Synthesis of 2D monolayer of graphene doped with hetero-atoms such as bor…
